dataset.title: Shop database
dataset.description: A small online-shop database with customers, products, orders and order_items. Every exercise runs against this data.

modules.title: SQL Foundations
modules.summary: Pull data out of a single table.

lessons.title: SELECT and FROM
lessons.objective: Choose which columns to return from a table.

exercise.prompt: Retrieve the name and city of every customer.
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name, city FROM customers;

lessons.title: SELECT DISTINCT
lessons.objective: Remove duplicate rows from a result.

exercise.prompt: List each distinct country that customers come from (one row per country).
exercise.starter_sql: SELECT country FROM customers;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM customers;

lessons.title: LIMIT
lessons.objective: Return only the first N rows.

exercise.prompt: Show the first 3 products ordered by id (return all columns).
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY id LIMIT 3;

modules.title: Filtering Rows
modules.summary: Keep only the rows you care about with WHERE.

lessons.title: The WHERE clause
lessons.objective: Filter rows with a single condition.

exercise.prompt: Find all products in the 'Electronics' category. Return name and price.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT name, price FROM products
WHERE ;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics';

lessons.title: AND / OR
lessons.objective: Combine multiple conditions.

exercise.prompt: Find Electronics products that cost less than 100. Return name and price.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT name, price FROM products
WHERE ;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics' AND price < 100;

lessons.title: BETWEEN, IN and LIKE
lessons.objective: Match ranges, lists and text patterns.

exercise.prompt: Find products whose price is between 5 and 50 (inclusive). Return name and price, cheapest first.
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE price BETWEEN 5 AND 50 ORDER BY price;

lessons.title: Filtering on numbers
lessons.objective: Use a numeric condition to find specific rows.

exercise.prompt: Find the products that are out of stock (stock equal to 0). Return the name only.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT name FROM products
WHERE ;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name FROM products WHERE stock = 0;

modules.title: Sorting & Shaping
modules.summary: Order results and compute new columns.

lessons.title: ORDER BY
lessons.objective: Sort results ascending or descending.

exercise.prompt: List all products from most to least expensive. Return name and price.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT name, price FROM products
ORDER BY ;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;

lessons.title: Column aliases & expressions
lessons.objective: Compute a value and give it a readable name.

exercise.prompt: For each product, show its name and its total inventory value (price multiplied by stock) as a column named inventory_value.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT name, price * stock AS
FROM products;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name, price * stock AS inventory_value FROM products;

modules.title: Aggregation
modules.summary: Summarise many rows into totals and averages.

lessons.title: COUNT
lessons.objective: Count rows in a table.

exercise.prompt: How many customers are there in total? Return a single value in a column named total_customers.
exercise.starter_sql: SELECT COUNT( ) AS total_customers FROM customers;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_customers FROM customers;

lessons.title: GROUP BY
lessons.objective: Aggregate within groups.

exercise.prompt: Count how many products are in each category. Return category and a column named product_count.
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY category;

lessons.title: SUM and AVG
lessons.objective: Compute totals and averages per group.

exercise.prompt: What is the average product price per category? Return category and a column named avg_price.
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT category, AVG(price) AS avg_price FROM products GROUP BY category;

lessons.title: HAVING
lessons.objective: Filter groups after aggregation.

exercise.prompt: Which categories contain more than 2 products? Return category and a column named product_count.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS product_count
FROM products
GROUP BY category
HAVING ;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 2;

modules.title: Joins
modules.summary: Combine rows from more than one table.

lessons.title: INNER JOIN
lessons.objective: Match rows across two related tables.

exercise.prompt: List every order id together with the name of the customer who placed it. Return order id and customer name.
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT orders.id, customers.name FROM orders INNER JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id;

lessons.title: LEFT JOIN
lessons.objective: Keep every row from the left table, even without a match.

exercise.prompt: Show every customer's name and their number of orders (0 if they never ordered) in a column named order_count.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id
GROUP BY ;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name;

lessons.title: Joining three tables
lessons.objective: Chain joins across several tables.

exercise.prompt: For each order item, show the order id, the product name, and the line total (quantity multiplied by unit_price) as a column named line_total.
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT oi.order_id, p.name AS product_name, oi.quantity * oi.unit_price AS line_total FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id;

modules.title: Subqueries
modules.summary: Use the result of one query inside another.

lessons.title: Subquery in WHERE
lessons.objective: Compare each row against an aggregate.

exercise.prompt: Find products that are more expensive than the average product price. Return name and price.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT name, price FROM products
WHERE price > (SELECT  FROM products);
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE price > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM products);

lessons.title: Ranking with a subquery
lessons.objective: Combine grouping, ordering and limiting to find a top result.

exercise.prompt: Find the customer who has placed the most orders. Return the customer's name and a column named order_count.
exercise.starter_sql:
SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id
GROUP BY c.id, c.name
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT ;
exercise.solution_sql: SELECT c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 1;

modules.title: Python Foundations
modules.summary: Learn core Python by writing and running real code.

lessons.title: print() and variables
lessons.objective: Store a value in a variable and print it to the screen.

exercise.prompt: Create a variable named name set to 'Ada', then print exactly: Hello, Ada!
exercise.starter_sql:
name = 
print()
exercise.solution_sql:
name = 'Ada'
print(f'Hello, {name}!')

lessons.title: if / else
lessons.objective: Branch program logic based on a condition.

exercise.prompt: Set age = 20. If age is 18 or older print 'adult', otherwise print 'minor'.

lessons.title: for loops
lessons.objective: Repeat an action for every item in a collection.

exercise.prompt: Print the numbers 1 to 5, one per line, using a for loop and range().
exercise.starter_sql:
for i in range():
    print(i)
exercise.solution_sql:
for i in range(1, 6):
    print(i)

lessons.title: Lists
lessons.objective: Store, iterate and transform a collection of values.

exercise.prompt: Given fruits = ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry'], print each fruit in uppercase, one per line.

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.objective: Package reusable logic into a function.

exercise.prompt: Write a function square(n) that returns n squared, then print square(6).

lessons.title: Dictionaries
lessons.objective: Look up values by key in a dictionary.

exercise.prompt: Given ages = {'Sam': 21, 'Lee': 34, 'Kai': 28}, print each name and age like 'Sam is 21', one per line, in the dict's insertion order.
